applicationInfo.name: Threat Composer
applicationInfo.description:
### Summary
Comprehensive threat modeling ecosystem with web app, VS Code extension, browser extension, and AI-powered CLI/MCP server for systematic security analysis.

### Key objectives
- Reduce time-to-value for threat modeling
- Support iterative, non-linear threat modeling processes
- Provide structured threat identification using Threat Grammar
- Enable automated threat model generation from source code
- Support multiple deployment and workflow options

### Use-cases
- Manual threat modeling through web interface
- IDE-integrated threat modeling via VS Code extension
- Browser-based threat modeling with extension
- Automated threat model generation from codebases
- Educational threat modeling workshops and training

### Features
- Structured threat statement composition using Threat Grammar
- Architecture and data flow diagram editors
- Threat and mitigation management with linking
- Insights dashboard for coverage analysis
- Multiple export formats (JSON, Markdown, DOCX, PDF)
- Example threat models and threat packs
- AI-powered automated threat model generation
- Version control integration support
- Workspace management and collaboration features

architecture.description:
### High Level Architecture

#### Application type
Multi-component threat modeling platform with web app, browser extension, VS Code extension, and AI CLI

#### Layers
- Presentation layer: React web app, browser extension UI, VS Code extension UI
- Business logic layer: TypeScript threat modeling engine, Python AI agents
- Data layer: Local storage, file-based persistence, JSON schema validation
- Infrastructure layer: AWS CDK deployment, CloudFront distribution, S3 hosting

#### Boundaries
- Client-side applications (web, browser extension, VS Code)
- AI processing boundary (Python MCP server with Amazon Bedrock)
- AWS cloud infrastructure boundary
- External repository integrations (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket)

#### Technology components
- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, CloudScape Design System
- Backend: Node.js, Python 3.10+, AWS CDK
- AI: Amazon Bedrock, Strands Agents framework
- Infrastructure: AWS CloudFront, S3, Lambda@Edge
- Build: Nx monorepo, Projen, Yarn workspaces

#### Deployment architecture
- Static website hosting via AWS CloudFront and S3
- GitHub Pages hosting option for public deployment
- Docker containerization with Nginx for self-hosting
- Browser extension distribution via web stores
- VS Code extension via marketplace

#### APIs
| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| MCP Server | JSON-RPC | AI threat modeling automation |
| File I/O | Local | Threat model persistence |
| AWS Bedrock | REST | AI model inference |
| Browser APIs | Native | Repository integration |

#### Security relevant components
- JSON schema validation for threat model data
- Content Security Policy headers
- CORS configuration for cross-origin requests
- AWS IAM roles for Bedrock access
- Local file system access controls

### Low Level Architecture
- React component library (@aws/threat-composer) for UI primitives
- Context providers for state management (Application, Architecture, Threats, Mitigations)
- Python agent orchestration system with multi-agent workflows
- File-based session management with incremental execution support
- JSON schema-based data validation and serialization
- CloudFront distribution with Lambda@Edge for request processing
- Browser content script injection for repository integration
- VS Code extension host integration with webview panels
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for AI tool integration

dataflow.description:
### Human actors
- Threat modelers using web interface for manual threat analysis
- Developers using VS Code extension for IDE-integrated threat modeling
- Security researchers using browser extension for repository analysis
- AI users leveraging CLI and MCP server for automated threat generation
- DevOps engineers deploying and configuring threat modeling infrastructure

### External entities
- GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket repositories for source code integration
- Amazon Bedrock for AI model inference and threat generation
- NPM registry for package dependencies and distribution
- Web browsers providing local storage and extension runtime
- AWS services (CloudFront, S3, Lambda@Edge) for hosting infrastructure

### Processes
- React web application providing threat modeling UI and workflow management
- Python AI agents performing automated codebase analysis and threat generation
- Browser extension content scripts enabling repository integration
- VS Code extension host providing IDE-integrated threat modeling interface
- AWS CDK deployment process managing cloud infrastructure provisioning
- JSON schema validation engine ensuring data integrity and format compliance
- MCP server orchestrating AI tool interactions and workflow execution

### Data stores
| Store | Type | High-Value (Y/N) |
|-------|------|-----------------|
| Browser local storage | Client-side | Y |
| File system threat models | Local files | Y |
| NPM package registry | External | N |
| GitHub repository | External | Y |
| AWS S3 static assets | Cloud storage | N |

### Trust boundaries
| Zone | Elements | Trust Level |
|------|----------|-------------|
| Client browser | Web app, extensions, local storage | Medium |
| Developer workstation | VS Code extension, file system | High |
| AWS cloud infrastructure | CloudFront, S3, Lambda@Edge | High |
| External repositories | GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket | Medium |
| AI processing | Python agents, Bedrock API | Medium |

### Data flows
| Flow ID | Description | Source | Target | Trust Boundaries Crossed | Assets |
|---------|-------------|--------|--------|-----------------------|--------|
| DF-01 | User loads web app | Browser | CloudFront/S3 | Client to Cloud | Static assets |
| DF-02 | Threat model creation | User | Web app | None | Threat data |
| DF-03 | Local storage persistence | Web app | Browser storage | None | Threat models |
| DF-04 | File export/import | Web app | File system | Client to Local | JSON/PDF/DOCX |
| DF-05 | Repository integration | Extension | GitHub API | Client to External | Source code |
| DF-06 | AI threat generation | CLI/MCP | Bedrock API | Local to AI | Code analysis |
| DF-07 | Extension installation | User | Browser/VS Code | Client to Extension | Extension code |
| DF-08 | Schema validation | App | JSON validator | None | Data integrity |
| DF-09 | CDK deployment | DevOps | AWS services | Local to Cloud | Infrastructure |
| DF-10 | Package distribution | Build | NPM registry | Internal to External | NPM packages |

assumptions.content: Analysis based on available documentation and source code structure

metadata.value: Comprehensive analysis performed on README files, package configurations, and source code structure to determine application characteristics

assumptions.content: Technology stack includes React, TypeScript, AWS CDK, and Python components

metadata.value: Based on package.json files and project structure analysis across multiple packages

assumptions.content: Monorepo structure with multiple deployment targets and integration points

metadata.value: Project contains web app, browser extension, VS Code extension, and AI CLI components in separate packages

assumptions.content: AI capabilities are experimental and use Amazon Bedrock for automated threat model generation

metadata.value: AI package documentation indicates experimental status with multi-agent architecture for threat modeling automation

assumptions.content: Application supports both hosted (GitHub Pages) and self-hosted (AWS) deployment models

metadata.value: Documentation describes multiple deployment options including GitHub Pages hosting and AWS CDK-based self-hosting

assumptions.content: Analysis based on monorepo structure with 5 main packages and comprehensive documentation

metadata.value: Architecture determined from package.json files, source code structure, and build configurations across threat-composer, threat-composer-app, threat-composer-infra, threat-composer-app-browser-extension, and threat-composer-ai packages

assumptions.content: Multi-deployment model supports GitHub Pages, AWS CDK, Docker, and local development

metadata.value: Based on application-stack.ts CDK infrastructure, Dockerfile configuration, and build scripts supporting multiple deployment targets

assumptions.content: AI components use experimental multi-agent architecture with Amazon Bedrock integration

metadata.value: Python-based AI package uses Strands Agents framework with MCP server for tool integration and Bedrock for LLM inference

assumptions.content: Frontend uses React 18 with CloudScape Design System and context-based state management

metadata.value: Based on package dependencies and component structure showing extensive use of AWS CloudScape components and React context providers

assumptions.content: Data persistence uses local storage and file-based JSON with strict schema validation

metadata.value: Architecture supports local storage contexts and file-based threat model persistence with JSON schema validation using threat-composer-v1.schema.json

assumptions.content: Data flow analysis based on comprehensive source code review and architecture documentation

metadata.value: Analysis performed on React components, Python agents, browser extensions, and AWS infrastructure code

assumptions.content: Trust boundaries identified based on deployment architecture and component interactions

metadata.value: Trust levels assigned based on control mechanisms and security configurations in each zone

assumptions.content: Data flows represent primary interaction patterns between system components

metadata.value: Flows identified from component communication patterns and API integrations

assumptions.content: AI components are experimental and may have different security characteristics

metadata.value: Python AI agents and MCP server represent newer experimental functionality with evolving security model

assumptions.content: Browser extensions require additional security considerations due to elevated permissions

metadata.value: Extensions have access to repository content and local storage with cross-origin capabilities

assumptions.content: STRIDE analysis focused on HIGH and MEDIUM priority threats with realistic attack vectors

metadata.value: Analysis prioritized threats with clear attack paths and significant business impact, avoiding theoretical or low-impact scenarios

assumptions.content: AI components are experimental and may have evolving security characteristics

metadata.value: AI-powered threat modeling features are marked as experimental and may require additional security considerations as they mature

assumptions.content: Browser extensions have elevated permissions that could be exploited

metadata.value: Browser and VS Code extensions require elevated permissions to access repositories and file systems, creating additional attack surface

assumptions.content: Supply chain attacks are a significant risk due to multiple distribution channels

metadata.value: Application is distributed through NPM, browser extension stores, VS Code marketplace, and AWS infrastructure, creating multiple potential compromise points

assumptions.content: Client-side data storage contains sensitive architectural and threat modeling information

metadata.value: Browser local storage and exported files may contain detailed architectural information and security assessments that require protection

mitigations.content: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers with strict-dynamic and nonce-based script loading to prevent XSS execution

metadata.value: Configure CSP headers in CloudFront/Lambda@Edge with strict-dynamic directive and implement nonce-based script loading in React components. Include script-src 'strict-dynamic' 'nonce-{random}' and object-src 'none' directives.

mitigations.content: Sanitize all user input using DOMPurify before rendering in React components, especially in threat description and metadata fields

metadata.value: Integrate DOMPurify library to sanitize HTML content before rendering. Apply sanitization to all user-controlled fields including threat statements, descriptions, assumptions, and mitigation content. Configure DOMPurify with strict settings to remove all script tags and event handlers.

mitigations.content: Implement cryptographic signatures for exported threat model files using JSON Web Signatures (JWS) to ensure integrity

metadata.value: Generate JWS signatures for exported JSON files using a private key. Include signature verification in the import process to detect tampering. Store public keys securely and implement key rotation procedures.

mitigations.content: Add file hash verification for imported threat model files to detect unauthorized modifications

metadata.value: Calculate SHA-256 hashes of exported files and store them separately. During import, recalculate hashes and compare to detect modifications. Display hash verification status to users during import process.

mitigations.content: Implement browser local storage encryption using Web Crypto API to protect stored threat model data

metadata.value: Encrypt threat model data before storing in browser local storage using AES-GCM with keys derived from user session. Implement secure key management and automatic decryption on data retrieval. Use PBKDF2 for key derivation with sufficient iterations.

mitigations.content: Configure AWS IAM roles with least privilege principles and implement MFA for all AWS account access

metadata.value: Create specific IAM roles for CloudFront, S3, and Lambda@Edge with minimal required permissions. Enforce MFA for all AWS console and API access. Implement AWS CloudTrail logging for all API calls and set up alerts for suspicious activities.

mitigations.content: Enable AWS CloudTrail and CloudWatch monitoring for S3 bucket access and CloudFront distribution changes

metadata.value: Configure CloudTrail to log all S3 API calls and CloudFront configuration changes. Set up CloudWatch alarms for unauthorized access attempts, unusual data access patterns, and infrastructure modifications. Implement automated incident response for critical alerts.

mitigations.content: Implement input validation and rate limiting for AI MCP server to prevent prompt injection and abuse

metadata.value: Validate all inputs to MCP server using strict schemas and content filters. Implement rate limiting per client/session to prevent abuse. Add prompt injection detection using pattern matching for common attack vectors. Log all suspicious inputs for analysis.

mitigations.content: Configure Amazon Bedrock API rate limits and implement exponential backoff with circuit breaker patterns

metadata.value: Set appropriate rate limits in Bedrock API calls and implement exponential backoff for failed requests. Add circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures. Monitor API usage patterns and implement alerting for unusual activity.

mitigations.content: Implement code signing for VS Code extension using Microsoft's signing infrastructure and enable automatic updates

metadata.value: Sign VS Code extension packages using Microsoft's official signing process. Enable automatic signature verification during installation. Implement automated update mechanisms to ensure users receive legitimate updates quickly.

mitigations.content: Implement browser extension content script isolation and minimize required permissions to essential APIs only

metadata.value: Review and minimize browser extension permissions to only those required for core functionality. Implement content script isolation to prevent access to page context. Use message passing for secure communication between content scripts and background scripts.

mitigations.content: Establish official publisher verification for browser extensions and implement brand protection monitoring

metadata.value: Complete publisher verification process with browser extension stores. Implement automated monitoring for impersonation attempts using brand names or similar icons. Set up alerts for unauthorized extensions using similar names or descriptions.

mitigations.content: Implement file integrity monitoring for JSON schema files with checksums and digital signatures

metadata.value: Generate checksums for JSON schema files and verify integrity before validation operations. Implement digital signatures for schema files to prevent tampering. Monitor schema file access and modifications with alerting.

mitigations.content: Enable NPM package signing and implement automated security scanning for all published packages

metadata.value: Configure NPM package signing using npm provenance and sigstore. Implement automated security scanning with tools like npm audit and Snyk before publishing. Set up monitoring for unauthorized package publications.

mitigations.content: Implement multi-factor authentication and code review requirements for NPM publishing workflows

metadata.value: Require MFA for all NPM publishing accounts and implement mandatory code review for package updates. Use automated CI/CD pipelines with security checks and approval gates. Implement emergency revocation procedures for compromised packages.

mitigations.content: Configure Subresource Integrity (SRI) hashes for all external JavaScript and CSS resources

metadata.value: Generate and implement SRI hashes for all external dependencies loaded by the web application. Automate SRI hash generation in build process and implement fallback mechanisms for hash verification failures.

mitigations.content: Implement Infrastructure as Code (IaC) security scanning and peer review for all CDK changes

metadata.value: Integrate security scanning tools like Checkov or AWS Config Rules into CDK deployment pipeline. Require peer review for all infrastructure changes and implement automated security policy validation before deployment.

mitigations.content: Configure API rate limiting and authentication for repository integration endpoints

metadata.value: Implement rate limiting for repository API calls with exponential backoff. Use OAuth tokens with appropriate scopes and implement token refresh mechanisms. Monitor API usage patterns and implement abuse detection.

mitigations.content: Implement client-side resource limits and pagination for large threat models to prevent browser crashes

metadata.value: Set maximum limits for threat model components (threats, mitigations, assumptions) and implement pagination for large datasets. Add client-side memory monitoring and graceful degradation when limits are approached.

mitigations.content: Configure document export with metadata sanitization to remove sensitive architectural details

metadata.value: Implement metadata scrubbing for PDF and DOCX exports to remove internal system details, file paths, and user information. Add user controls for export sensitivity levels and implement watermarking for sensitive documents.

mitigations.content: Implement secure configuration management for AI agent prompts with version control and approval workflows

metadata.value: Store AI agent configurations in version control with mandatory review processes. Implement configuration validation and rollback capabilities. Add audit logging for all configuration changes and implement automated testing for prompt modifications.

mitigations.content: Configure CloudFront with AWS WAF rules to prevent cache poisoning and implement cache key normalization

metadata.value: Deploy AWS WAF with CloudFront to filter malicious requests and implement cache key normalization to prevent cache poisoning attacks. Configure appropriate cache headers and implement cache invalidation procedures.

mitigations.content: Implement browser extension permission auditing and runtime permission monitoring

metadata.value: Monitor browser extension permission usage at runtime and alert on unexpected permission requests. Implement periodic permission audits and user notifications for permission changes. Add telemetry for permission usage patterns.

mitigations.content: Configure secure logging for MCP server with log sanitization to prevent sensitive data exposure

metadata.value: Implement log sanitization to remove sensitive information like source code snippets and architectural details from MCP server logs. Configure log retention policies and implement secure log storage with access controls.

threats.prerequisites: with network access to the web application
threats.threatAction: inject malicious scripts through stored XSS vulnerabilities in threat model data fields
threats.threatImpact: execution of arbitrary JavaScript in other users' browsers when they view the threat model
threats.statement: A malicious actor with network access to the web application can inject malicious scripts through stored XSS vulnerabilities in threat model data fields, which leads to execution of arbitrary JavaScript in other users' browsers when they view the threat model, resulting in reduced confidentiality of user sessions, threat model data, browser local storage

metadata.value: React application processes user input in threat descriptions, assumptions, and other text fields. Without proper sanitization, malicious scripts could be stored and executed when other users view the data.

threats.prerequisites: with access to exported threat model files
threats.threatAction: modify JSON threat model files to inject malicious data or alter threat assessments
threats.threatImpact: compromised threat model integrity leading to incorrect security decisions
threats.statement: An attacker with access to exported threat model files can modify JSON threat model files to inject malicious data or alter threat assessments, which leads to compromised threat model integrity leading to incorrect security decisions, resulting in reduced integrity of threat model data, security assessments

metadata.value: JSON files are exported and imported without cryptographic signatures or integrity checks. Attackers could modify files to hide threats or inject false information.

threats.prerequisites: installed on the same browser
threats.threatAction: access and exfiltrate threat model data from local storage
threats.threatImpact: unauthorized disclosure of sensitive threat modeling information
threats.statement: A malicious browser extension installed on the same browser can access and exfiltrate threat model data from local storage, which leads to unauthorized disclosure of sensitive threat modeling information, resulting in reduced confidentiality of threat model data, security assessments, architectural information

metadata.value: Browser local storage is accessible to other extensions with appropriate permissions. Threat models may contain sensitive architectural information.

threats.prerequisites: with compromised AWS credentials
threats.threatAction: modify or delete S3-hosted static assets and CloudFront distributions
threats.threatImpact: service disruption or malicious code injection into the web application
threats.statement: An attacker with compromised AWS credentials can modify or delete S3-hosted static assets and CloudFront distributions, which leads to service disruption or malicious code injection into the web application, resulting in reduced availability of web application, static assets, user sessions

metadata.value: AWS infrastructure hosts the web application. Compromised credentials could allow attackers to replace legitimate assets with malicious ones or cause service outages.

threats.prerequisites: with access to the AI MCP server
threats.threatAction: inject malicious prompts to manipulate AI-generated threat models
threats.threatImpact: generation of biased or incomplete threat assessments
threats.statement: A malicious actor with access to the AI MCP server can inject malicious prompts to manipulate AI-generated threat models, which leads to generation of biased or incomplete threat assessments, resulting in reduced integrity of AI-generated threats, threat model accuracy

metadata.value: AI components are experimental and may be vulnerable to prompt injection attacks that could bias threat generation or cause the AI to miss critical threats.

threats.prerequisites: with network access to Amazon Bedrock API
threats.threatAction: perform denial of service attacks against AI inference endpoints
threats.threatImpact: unavailability of automated threat modeling capabilities
threats.statement: An attacker with network access to Amazon Bedrock API can perform denial of service attacks against AI inference endpoints, which leads to unavailability of automated threat modeling capabilities, resulting in reduced availability of AI threat generation, automated analysis

metadata.value: AI components rely on external Bedrock API. High-volume requests or API abuse could exhaust rate limits or cause service disruption.

threats.prerequisites: of a repository being analyzed
threats.threatAction: embed malicious code or misleading comments to influence AI threat analysis
threats.threatImpact: generation of inaccurate threat models that miss real vulnerabilities
threats.statement: A malicious repository owner of a repository being analyzed can embed malicious code or misleading comments to influence AI threat analysis, which leads to generation of inaccurate threat models that miss real vulnerabilities, resulting in reduced integrity of threat analysis accuracy, security assessments

metadata.value: AI agents analyze source code from repositories. Malicious code or comments could be crafted to mislead the AI analysis and hide actual security issues.

threats.prerequisites: with access to VS Code extension marketplace
threats.threatAction: distribute malicious versions of the threat composer extension
threats.threatImpact: compromise of developer workstations and theft of source code
threats.statement: An attacker with access to VS Code extension marketplace can distribute malicious versions of the threat composer extension, which leads to compromise of developer workstations and theft of source code, resulting in reduced confidentiality of developer workstations, source code, threat models

metadata.value: VS Code extensions have elevated permissions and access to workspace files. A compromised extension could steal sensitive code or inject malicious functionality.

threats.prerequisites: with access to browser extension web store
threats.threatAction: upload a malicious extension impersonating the threat composer browser extension
threats.threatImpact: users installing fake extensions that steal repository access tokens
threats.statement: A malicious actor with access to browser extension web store can upload a malicious extension impersonating the threat composer browser extension, which leads to users installing fake extensions that steal repository access tokens, resulting in reduced confidentiality of repository access tokens, source code, user credentials

metadata.value: Browser extensions can access repository APIs and user tokens. Fake extensions could harvest credentials and access private repositories.

threats.prerequisites: with file system access
threats.threatAction: modify JSON schema validation files to bypass security checks
threats.threatImpact: acceptance of malformed or malicious threat model data
threats.statement: An attacker with file system access can modify JSON schema validation files to bypass security checks, which leads to acceptance of malformed or malicious threat model data, resulting in reduced integrity of data validation, threat model integrity

metadata.value: JSON schema files define data validation rules. If compromised, they could allow malicious data to pass validation checks.

threats.prerequisites: with access to NPM publishing credentials
threats.threatAction: publish compromised versions of threat composer packages
threats.threatImpact: widespread distribution of malicious code to downstream users
threats.statement: A malicious insider with access to NPM publishing credentials can publish compromised versions of threat composer packages, which leads to widespread distribution of malicious code to downstream users, resulting in reduced integrity of NPM packages, user installations, threat modeling data

metadata.value: NPM packages are distributed to users who install the threat composer components. Compromised packages could affect many installations.

threats.prerequisites: with network access to the web application
threats.threatAction: exploit client-side vulnerabilities to access local storage data
threats.threatImpact: unauthorized access to stored threat models and sensitive architectural information
threats.statement: An attacker with network access to the web application can exploit client-side vulnerabilities to access local storage data, which leads to unauthorized access to stored threat models and sensitive architectural information, resulting in reduced confidentiality of threat models, architectural data, user sessions

metadata.value: Browser local storage contains threat modeling data that may include sensitive architectural information. Client-side attacks could expose this data.

threats.prerequisites: with access to AWS CDK deployment process
threats.threatAction: modify infrastructure code to create backdoors or weaken security controls
threats.threatImpact: persistent compromise of the hosting infrastructure
threats.statement: A malicious actor with access to AWS CDK deployment process can modify infrastructure code to create backdoors or weaken security controls, which leads to persistent compromise of the hosting infrastructure, resulting in reduced confidentiality of AWS infrastructure, web application, user data

metadata.value: CDK code defines the AWS infrastructure. Malicious modifications could introduce security weaknesses or backdoors that persist across deployments.

threats.prerequisites: with access to repository integration APIs
threats.threatAction: abuse API rate limits to cause denial of service for repository analysis
threats.threatImpact: inability to perform automated threat modeling on repositories
threats.statement: An attacker with access to repository integration APIs can abuse API rate limits to cause denial of service for repository analysis, which leads to inability to perform automated threat modeling on repositories, resulting in reduced availability of repository integration, automated analysis

metadata.value: Repository integrations rely on external APIs with rate limits. Abuse could exhaust quotas and prevent legitimate analysis activities.

threats.prerequisites: with access to the web application
threats.threatAction: create extremely large threat models to consume excessive browser resources
threats.threatImpact: browser crashes or performance degradation for other users
threats.statement: A malicious user with access to the web application can create extremely large threat models to consume excessive browser resources, which leads to browser crashes or performance degradation for other users, resulting in reduced availability of web application performance, user experience

metadata.value: Large threat models with many components could consume excessive memory or processing power in the browser, affecting application performance.

threats.prerequisites: with access to exported PDF or DOCX files
threats.threatAction: extract sensitive architectural information from document metadata
threats.threatImpact: unauthorized disclosure of system architecture details
threats.statement: An attacker with access to exported PDF or DOCX files can extract sensitive architectural information from document metadata, which leads to unauthorized disclosure of system architecture details, resulting in reduced confidentiality of architectural information, system design details

metadata.value: Exported documents may contain metadata or embedded information that reveals more than intended about system architecture and security measures.

threats.prerequisites: with access to AI agent configuration
threats.threatAction: modify agent prompts or workflows to generate misleading threat assessments
threats.threatImpact: systematic bias in automated threat identification leading to security gaps
threats.statement: A malicious actor with access to AI agent configuration can modify agent prompts or workflows to generate misleading threat assessments, which leads to systematic bias in automated threat identification leading to security gaps, resulting in reduced integrity of AI threat generation, security assessments

metadata.value: AI agents use configurable prompts and workflows. Malicious modifications could systematically bias threat generation to hide certain types of vulnerabilities.

threats.prerequisites: with network access to CloudFront distribution
threats.threatAction: perform cache poisoning attacks to serve malicious content
threats.threatImpact: distribution of compromised web application code to users
threats.statement: An attacker with network access to CloudFront distribution can perform cache poisoning attacks to serve malicious content, which leads to distribution of compromised web application code to users, resulting in reduced integrity of web application, static assets, user sessions

metadata.value: CloudFront caching could be exploited to serve malicious content if cache poisoning vulnerabilities exist in the CDN configuration.

threats.prerequisites: with access to browser extension permissions
threats.threatAction: escalate privileges to access additional browser APIs and user data
threats.threatImpact: unauthorized access to browsing history, cookies, and other sensitive browser data
threats.statement: A malicious actor with access to browser extension permissions can escalate privileges to access additional browser APIs and user data, which leads to unauthorized access to browsing history, cookies, and other sensitive browser data, resulting in reduced confidentiality of user privacy, browser data, authentication tokens

metadata.value: Browser extensions may request minimal permissions initially but could exploit vulnerabilities to access additional browser APIs and sensitive user data.

threats.prerequisites: with access to MCP server logs
threats.threatAction: extract sensitive information from AI processing logs and debug output
threats.threatImpact: disclosure of source code snippets, architectural details, and threat analysis data
threats.statement: An attacker with access to MCP server logs can extract sensitive information from AI processing logs and debug output, which leads to disclosure of source code snippets, architectural details, and threat analysis data, resulting in reduced confidentiality of source code, architectural information, AI processing data

metadata.value: AI processing may log sensitive information during analysis. Logs could contain source code snippets, architectural details, or intermediate analysis results.
